author: qwen
description: Multilingual instruction-tuned model from the Qwen 2.5 family. Function calling, JSON-mode, 29 languages.
readmeMarkdown:
# Qwen 2.5 7B Instruct

Qwen 2.5 is the latest series of large language models from Alibaba's Qwen team. The 7B Instruct variant is post-trained for chat, function calling, and structured-output use cases.

## Highlights

- 29 languages supported, with notable improvements in coding and mathematics
- Native function calling and JSON-mode output
- 32K-token context, 8K-token generation length

Recommended for general-purpose chat workloads on consumer hardware.

author: qwen
description: Mid-size Qwen 2.5 dense instruct model. Stronger reasoning and code than the 7B at single-GPU cost.
readmeMarkdown:
# Qwen 2.5 14B Instruct

The 14B sibling of Qwen 2.5. Notable gains over the 7B on code, math, and long-form reasoning. Comfortable to run on a single 16GB+ GPU.

author: qwen
description: Flagship dense Qwen 2.5. Competitive with GPT-4-class models on instruction following and code.
readmeMarkdown:
# Qwen 2.5 32B Instruct

Flagship Qwen 2.5 dense model. ~24 GB free at Q4_K_M; runs comfortably on Apple Silicon with 32 GB unified memory.

author: qwen
description: Code-specialised Qwen 2.5. Trained on 5.5T code-heavy tokens. 128K context for whole-repo prompts.
readmeMarkdown:
# Qwen 2.5 Coder 7B Instruct

Code-tuned Qwen 2.5 with 128K context — comfortable swallowing whole repos. Strong at fill-in-the-middle and multi-language synthesis.

author: qwen
description: Reasoning-tuned Qwen3 4B. Emits a deliberation trace before answering — the 'thinking' variant.
readmeMarkdown:
# Qwen3 4B Thinking 2507

Updated thinking version of Qwen3 4B featuring continued reinforcement learning. Best for problems where step-by-step reasoning helps.

## Behaviour

The model emits a `<think>` block before the final answer. Universe parses this into a collapsible 'Thought for Xs' disclosure.

author: qwen
description: Next-gen Qwen3 dense 8B. Hybrid thinking/non-thinking modes, native 32K context.
readmeMarkdown:
# Qwen3 8B

The next Qwen generation. Toggleable thinking via the reasoning chat tag — same model serves both fast-chat and deliberate-reasoning use cases.

author: qwen
description: Vision-language Qwen2. Dynamic resolution, strong on chart, document, and screen understanding.
readmeMarkdown:
# Qwen2-VL 7B Instruct

Multimodal Qwen2. Pass images alongside text prompts. Handles arbitrary resolutions natively (no resize to 224x224).

author: meta-llama
description: Meta's open-weight 8B instruction model. 128K context, tool use, multilingual.
readmeMarkdown:
# Llama 3.1 8B Instruct

Meta's open-weight instruction-tuned model. 128K-token context, supports tool calling, eight languages (en, fr, de, es, it, pt, hi, th).

Released under the Llama 3.1 Community Licence.

author: meta-llama
description: Meta's open-weight 70B flagship. Runs on serious hardware (~42 GB at Q4); near GPT-4 quality.
readmeMarkdown:
# Llama 3.1 70B Instruct

Meta's 70B flagship from the Llama 3.1 family. ~42 GB free at Q4_K_M.

author: meta-llama
description: Edge-tier Llama 3.2. ~800 MB at Q4 — runs on phones, laptops, anything with 2 GB free.
readmeMarkdown:
# Llama 3.2 1B Instruct

For edge devices. Surprisingly capable at chitchat and simple tools. 128K context inherited from the Llama 3 architecture.

author: meta-llama
description: Small Llama 3.2 for fast inference. The practical default for on-device assistant work.
readmeMarkdown:
# Llama 3.2 3B Instruct

The practical default for fast on-device chat. 128K context, tool calling, eight languages.

author: meta-llama
description: Meta's open-weight multimodal model. Image + text in, text out. Documents, charts, OCR.
readmeMarkdown:
# Llama 3.2 11B Vision Instruct

Open-weight multimodal. Image understanding, charts, OCR. Vision adapter sits on top of the Llama 3.1 8B text backbone with additional vision layers (~3B).

author: meta-llama
description: Late-2024 refresh of Llama 70B. Closer to 405B quality at 70B compute.
readmeMarkdown:
# Llama 3.3 70B Instruct

December-2024 refresh of the Llama 3.1 70B. Big quality jump — Meta's published evals put it close to the (much larger) 405B in chat and instruction following.

author: google
description: Tiny Gemma 2 instruct. Punches above its weight via large-model distillation.
readmeMarkdown:
# Gemma 2 2B Instruct

Google's edge-friendly chat model from the Gemma 2 family. Trained via distillation from larger Gemma siblings.

author: google
description: Google's Gemma 2 9B instruction-tuned. Conservative outputs, good for production.
readmeMarkdown:
# Gemma 2 9B Instruct

Well-aligned, conservative open chat model from Google DeepMind. 8K context, but high-quality outputs for that window.

author: google
description: Larger Gemma 2 — competitive with 70B-class chat models at lower compute.
readmeMarkdown:
# Gemma 2 27B Instruct

Larger Gemma; per Google's evals comparable to Llama 3 70B on many chat benchmarks at roughly a third the active compute.

author: microsoft
description: 3.8B Phi-3 Mini. Trained on heavily-filtered synthetic + web data; punches above its weight.
readmeMarkdown:
# Phi-3 Mini 4K Instruct

Microsoft's small reasoning-tuned model. Trained with a heavy focus on textbook-style data — excellent quality-per-parameter on reasoning and math benchmarks.

author: microsoft
description: Phi-3.5 Mini — 3.8B with 128K context. Improved tool use and multilingual coverage over Phi-3.
readmeMarkdown:
# Phi-3.5 Mini Instruct

A refresh of Phi-3 Mini with 128K context and broader multilingual coverage. Strong on instruction following and tool calling.

author: microsoft
description: Phi-4 14B — Microsoft's late-2024 flagship. Heavy emphasis on reasoning, math, and code.
readmeMarkdown:
# Phi-4

Microsoft's late-2024 flagship. Trained with synthetic reasoning data. Phi-4 is 14B parameters with a 16K context window — small but very strong on reasoning and code evals.

author: mistralai
description: The canonical 7B chat baseline. v0.3 adds function calling and an expanded 32K-token vocab.
readmeMarkdown:
# Mistral 7B Instruct v0.3

The canonical 7B chat baseline. Many of the finetunes you'll see in the wild build on this. v0.3 adds function-calling and a 32K-token tokenizer.

author: mistralai
description: Mistral + NVIDIA collaboration. 12B params, 128K context, multilingual, strong tool calling.
readmeMarkdown:
# Mistral Nemo Instruct 2407

Drop-in 12B chat model with 128K context and strong multilingual coverage. Built jointly with NVIDIA on the Tekken tokenizer.

author: mistralai
description: Sparse mixture-of-experts: 47B total params, ~13B active per token.
readmeMarkdown:
# Mixtral 8x7B Instruct v0.1

Mixture-of-experts. 47B total / ~13B active per token. Often runs near a dense 30-40B's quality at much lower per-token compute, at the cost of higher VRAM.

author: mistralai
description: Mistral's code-specialised dense 22B. 80+ languages, fill-in-the-middle, 32K context.
readmeMarkdown:
# Codestral 22B v0.1

Code-focused Mistral. Fill-in-the-middle + chat in one model. 80+ programming languages.

author: deepseek-ai
description: DeepSeek R1's reasoning behaviour distilled into a Llama 3.1 8B body. Top open reasoning model in its class.
readmeMarkdown:
# DeepSeek R1 Distill Llama 8B

Reasoning distillation: DeepSeek-R1's behaviour, Llama's compute profile. Emits a `<think>` block before the final answer.

author: deepseek-ai
description: R1 reasoning distilled into Qwen 2.5 14B. Among the strongest open reasoning models in the 14B class.
readmeMarkdown:
# DeepSeek R1 Distill Qwen 14B

Top open reasoning 14B. Emits thinking traces before answers.

author: deepseek-ai
description: 16B MoE code model — 2.4B active params. Fast and capable, 128K context.
readmeMarkdown:
# DeepSeek Coder V2 Lite Instruct

MoE code model — small active footprint, big effective capacity. 128K context, 86+ programming languages.

author: NousResearch
description: Nous Research's instruction finetune of Llama 3.1 8B. Strong system-prompt adherence, agentic.
readmeMarkdown:
# Hermes 3 Llama 3.1 8B

Nous Research's open finetune of Llama 3.1 8B. Famously good at following system prompts and personas; popular for agentic and roleplay workloads.

author: liquid
description: Liquid AI's first-gen hybrid model for on-device deployment. Non-transformer architecture.
readmeMarkdown:
# LFM2 1.2B by LiquidAI

Hybrid architecture model intended for local use, by Liquid AI. Non-transformer (Liquid Neural Network) backbone — designed from the ground up for efficient on-device inference.

author: liquid
description: LFM2.5 — Liquid AI's updated hybrid model. On-device focused, agentic-ready, 32K context.
readmeMarkdown:
# LFM2.5 1.2B by LiquidAI

LFM2.5 is a new family of hybrid models designed for on-device deployment. It builds on the LFM2 device-optimized architecture and represents a significant leap forward in building reliable agents on the edge.

Supports a context length of 32k.

author: bigcode
description: BigCode's flagship code model. Trained on 600+ programming languages, permissive licence.
readmeMarkdown:
# StarCoder 2 15B

Open code model from BigCode (HuggingFace + ServiceNow). Trained on The Stack v2 — 600+ programming languages. Permissive (OpenRAIL-M) licence.

author: ibm-granite
description: IBM's enterprise-focused Granite 3.1. Apache 2.0, audited training data, 128K context.
readmeMarkdown:
# Granite 3.1 8B Instruct

IBM's open-data, enterprise-tuned chat model. Apache 2.0 — training data and process documented for enterprise audit.

author: allenai
description: Allen AI's fully open 13B. Data, code, and weights all permissively licensed.
readmeMarkdown:
# OLMo 2 13B Instruct

Fully open: training data, training code, intermediate checkpoints, and final weights all released.

author: 01-ai
description: 01.AI's large open chat model. Strong Chinese + English coverage, 4K context (Yi-1.5-34B-Chat-16K for long).
readmeMarkdown:
# Yi 1.5 34B Chat

01.AI's open chat model. Bilingual zh/en. Llama-style architecture.

author: CohereForAI
description: Cohere's RAG- and tool-tuned 35B. Optimised for grounded responses with inline citations.
readmeMarkdown:
# Command R 08-2024

Cohere's RAG-tuned open weights. Excellent citation behaviour, structured for retrieval-augmented generation. CC-BY-NC research licence.

author: TinyLlama
description: 1.1B chat baseline. Tiny enough to run anywhere — useful for testing pipelines and edge demos.
readmeMarkdown:
# TinyLlama 1.1B Chat v1.0

Really small. Good for smoke tests, RAG pipeline development, and edge experimentation.

author: HuggingFaceTB
description: HuggingFace's curated tiny chat model. 1.7B params, trained on a heavily-filtered corpus.
readmeMarkdown:
# SmolLM2 1.7B Instruct

HuggingFace's well-trained small chat model. Punches well above its size thanks to a curated dataset (FineWeb-Edu, math, code mix).

author: nomic-ai
description: Open 137M text embedding model. 8192 context, matryoshka-style truncatable embeddings.
readmeMarkdown:
# Nomic Embed Text v1.5

Open text embedding model with 8192 context. Matryoshka — embeddings can be truncated from 768 → 256 dims with minimal loss. Use for RAG / search / clustering.

author: BAAI
description: Beijing Academy of AI's high-quality English embedding model. 335M params, 512 context.
readmeMarkdown:
# BGE Large EN v1.5

Widely-used English embedding model from BAAI. Strong MTEB scores; the default for many production RAG stacks.

author: mixedbread-ai
description: Mixedbread's 335M English embedding model. Strong MTEB performance, Apache 2.0.
readmeMarkdown:
# mxbai-embed-large v1

Mixedbread AI's open English embedding model. Apache 2.0. Outperforms many larger embedding models on MTEB.

author: sentence-transformers
description: Tiny 384-dim embedding model — the default baseline in many RAG starter stacks.
readmeMarkdown:
# all-MiniLM-L6-v2

Very small embedding model. Fast, good enough as a baseline. 22M params, 384 dimensions.
